summary: Added a data-derived Expansion Realms layer that groups lanes into scalable game-like packs with avatar clusters, pack meters, clickable lane chips, and active-state sync.

queries: dashboard information architecture grouping many cards progressive disclosure patterns
game world map biome selection level packs UI pattern
scalable dashboard card grouping filters tabs large number of projects UX
pattern_notes: Progressive disclosure keeps the essential group overview visible while deferring lane-level details until requested.
Grouped navigation reduces scanning cost when a dashboard has many repeated cards or paths.
The Atlas pack layer avoids hidden carousel tabs; it keeps all current packs visible on desktop and bounded-scrollable on mobile.

ui_contract: Every current lane appears once as a lane chip inside a realm pack.
Packs are derived from lane metadata and do not require snapshot schema changes.
Each pack shows lane count, outcome count, blocker count, artifact count, total levels, and a progress meter.
Clicking a pack lead or lane chip syncs URL hash, lane detail, lane list, progression card, and active realm pack state.
Phone view uses a one-column bounded scroll deck; laptop uses three columns; wide desktop uses five columns.

checks.result: Expansion Realms panel visible, 5 packs rendered, 12 lane chips rendered, 4 summary stats rendered, one-column bounded scroll deck, no horizontal overflow, and no console errors.
Expansion Realms panel visible, 5 packs rendered, 12 lane chips rendered, three-column deck, no horizontal overflow, and no console errors.
Expansion Realms panel visible, 5 packs rendered, 12 lane chips rendered, five-column deck, no horizontal overflow, and no console errors.

Clicking the prediction_market_research lane chip updated the hash to #lane=prediction_market_research&view=overview and activated the matching realm chip, pack, progression card, lane-list row, and detail panel.
